items.title: Benchmark-Outcome Separation in Federal Independent Dispute Resolution: A Descriptive Analysis of Qualifying Payment Amount and Adjudicated Payment in Complex Repair Claims - Cureus
items.reason: This peer-reviewed descriptive analysis directly examines how the Qualifying Payment Amount (QPA) benchmark and IDR arbitrator adjudicated payments diverge in federal IDR cases, providing empirical data on IDR process outcomes that revenue-cycle teams use to understand payment predictability.
items.summary: Researchers published a descriptive analysis in Cureus examining the relationship between Qualifying Payment Amounts (QPAs) and arbitrator-adjudicated payments in complex repair claims processed through federal Independent Dispute Resolution. The study investigates whether QPA benchmarks systematically separate from final IDR payment outcomes, providing empirical evidence on how the federal IDR mechanism actually resolves out-of-network disputes in a specific claims category. This analysis is relevant to revenue-cycle teams managing IDR cases because it reveals patterns in how arbitrators' decisions deviate from the statutory QPA benchmark, informing expectations for dispute resolution outcomes and potential negotiation strategies in similar claim scenarios.

items.title: Kaiser Permanente to pay community hospital over $82M to resolve out-of-network reimbursement dispute - HealthExec
items.reason: This item directly reports a significant settlement resolving a payer-provider dispute over out-of-network reimbursement practices, which is a core example of the payer network practices litigation covered by the topic definition.
items.summary: Kaiser Permanente agreed to pay a community hospital over $82 million to settle an out-of-network reimbursement dispute. The settlement resolves litigation concerning Kaiser's reimbursement practices related to out-of-network provider status. This case exemplifies the type of payer-provider disputes central to network-affiliation reimbursement conflicts and demonstrates potential financial exposure for health plans from such practices. The resolution is relevant to revenue-cycle teams monitoring payer network policies and litigation trends that could affect facility reimbursement and contract negotiations.

also.title: Kaiser Health Plan ordered to pay hospital $82M over insufficient reimbursement
